A"1" on *ni"i'in" ."pi"s of the 6azette coulainittg; lhe said noiir,"riion were made available to the public; And whereas, copies of the said Gazette were rnade available tlt the public on the 6th MaY, 2005; And whereas no objections and suggesiions were received from the public in respect ot the said Order; Now,therefore,inexerciseofthepowersconferredbysub:,section (2) of section 27 rcad wiiii'Seatn zg of ttre lnsecticides Act' 1968' the 5#;"iA#;"It "tt"i*n=iaering the recommendations of the E4pert Group constituted for th;;u-i"0"os" ""9. after consultation witli the Regishation Committee t"t 'p uiJti tnesaid Act' and' on being satisfied that the use of Monocr"6h;t ;; vegetables..is likely to involve.lrealth ffi.;; t-n*"n u"ing",-[ureuy makis the following order natnelv . -- 2 of 3 -- I r{Fr I]-rqE-E 3 (n) l qI{iI CI {I6Fl? : oI{ITIIItET J 1 (1) This order may be called the Monocrotophos (cancellation of Ceftificate of Regiskation) Order, 2005, (2) lt shall come into force on and from the dak) of pr.ibtication of this Order in the Official Gazette. 2 (1) (2) (3) (4) The use of Monocrotophos on vegetables shall be banned on and from the date of publication of this Order; The certificates of registration granted for Monocrotophos shall be called back by the Registration Committee from all regiskants including new registrants for incorporation of the requirement of the waming in bold letters "BANNED FOR USE ON VEGETABLES.'on tabels and teaflets. ln respect of those registrants who do not return the registiation certificate, as per this Order within a period of six months with effect from the date of publication of this Order, their license granted under section 1 3 of the said Act shall not be renewed or action under Section 14 of the Act should be taken. The State Governments shall have power to take such steps under the relevant provisions of the said Act and the rules made thereunder as they may deem fit for the execution of these Orders in the State coneerned.
